definition: Form as the material or visible side of experience, especially in heap analysis, sense-field contexts, and the name-and-form pair.
notes: The project keeps form as the default because it works across the five-heaps family and the sixfold sense-field system without forcing a single modern theory of matter. Material form is useful when the physical side needs to be made explicit, but body is too narrow and matter is too reified for blanket use. In sense-field contexts rūpā often means visible forms, while in the five heaps rūpa marks the material side of the analysis more broadly. Translators should keep the heap, visible-object, and nāmarūpa uses distinct and should not let matter or body become automatic replacements. The OSF glossary confirms form as the house default, and SN 22.59 supports keeping that same default in the heap-family and not-self formulas.

context_rules.context: In most heap and doctrinal contexts
When the physical side of the contrast with nāma needs to be explicit
context_rules.notes: Use in explanatory prose or where plain form would be too thin.

context_rules.context: In eye-and-form sense-field contexts such as cakkhuñca paṭicca rūpe ca
context_rules.notes: Use the visual-object sense rather than the broader heap sense.

context_rules.context: In compounds such as rūpakkhandha and in the pattern yaṃ kiñci rūpaṃ
context_rules.notes: Keep continuity with the house treatment of khandha and the standard heap formulas.

example_phrases.pali: cakkhuñca paṭicca rūpe ca uppajjati cakkhuviññāṇaṃ
example_phrases.translation: dependent on eye and visible forms, eye-knowing arises
whatever form there is, past, future, or present
example_phrases.notes: Shows the canonical heap and not-self pattern.

version_notes: Strengthened to keep heap, visible-object, and name-and-form uses distinct while preserving one stable default in the five-heaps family.

authority_basis.scope: Supports keeping form as the default across heap, visible-object, and name-and-form contexts, with material form available when the physical side needs emphasis.
Supports the heap-family and not-self use of rūpa without flattening it into body or matter.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'form' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not let body or matter become automatic defaults, and do not carry the visible-object sense into the wider heap analysis unless the local line is explicitly visual.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift between heap-context, sense-field context, and name-and-form context for rūpa.
